title: The Next Bandon Dunes Just Opened
body:
The family behind Bandon Dunes and Sand Valley just did it again. This time in the piney woods of East Texas and the public gets their first crack at it this fall.

Wild Spring Dunes sits on 2,400 acres near Nacogdoches, Texas about two hours from both Dallas and Houston. Tom Doak designed the course, the same architect behind Cape Kidnappers in New Zealand which we covered last week.

The full 18 holes open to the public for the first time on September 8th and fall tee times just opened for reservation on June 15th. That means you can go book this right now as one of the first public players ever on this course.

## The Course
### Wild Spring Dunes

![Wild Spring Dunes (Official Website)](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1781103190/WSP_Dunes_1_djsrme.jpg)
*Wild Spring Dunes (Official Website)*

Wild Spring Dunes unfolds across four completely different landscapes on the same property. Towering pine and hardwood forests give way to open meadows, then rolling hills, then spring fed ravines carved over generations.

The site has 120 feet of elevation change which is rare for Texas and the sandy soils give the course a firm fast feel that reminds people of the Carolina Sandhills and Pinehurst region more than anything you picture when you think Texas golf.

Early reviews from founders who have played preview rounds describe it the same way people described Sand Valley and Bandon Dunes when those first opened. A place that feels completely removed from everything and completely worth the drive to get there.

This is a walking only course with caddies available. No carts. That is part of what makes it feel like a real golf destination rather than just another resort track.

## The Numbers
### One of the Best New Course Values

- September through November 2026: $275 per round, replay $140
- December 2026: $195 per round, replay $100
- 2027 rates: TBA

For a brand new Tom Doak design from the team behind Bandon Dunes, $275 is a genuinely strong value. The course will not be this accessible forever. Sand Valley and Bandon both got more expensive and harder to get on as word spread. The window to get in early on Wild Spring Dunes is right now.

[book-trip url="https://www.wildspringdunes.com/tee-time-assistant" title="Book This Trip" cta="See Details"]

Tee times are released in limited windows through their Tee Time Assistant. Join the request list with your preferred dates and they will notify you by email and text when those dates open up. When the notification hits, follow the link and secure your round before it fills. Availability is limited so staying on the list is the only way to guarantee you do not miss it.

## Where to Stay / Beyond the Golf
### This is Not City Golf

On site cabins are in development as the resort builds out. The nearest town is Nacogdoches about 30 minutes away, a small college town with a historic downtown, some restaurants, and the kind of laid back Texas hospitality you would expect.

The Fredonia Hotel is the best spot for dinner and drinks after your round. This is not a destination you go to for the nightlife and that is completely fine. Bandon Dunes is in the middle of nowhere Oregon and nobody complains.

You go for the golf, the land, and the quiet. If that crew is your crew, this trip is going to be one of the best you have ever taken.

## The Insider
### This Will Sell Out Fast


![Wild Spring Dunes (Official Course Website)](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1781103190/WSD_3_qrw4ol.jpg)
*Wild Spring Dunes (Official Course Website)*

Fall tee times opened June 15th and this course is already generating serious buzz in golf media. September and October are the sweet spot for weather in East Texas before the heat lingers too long into fall. 

Get on the tee sheet before everyone else figures this out. The early rounds at Sand Valley and Bandon sold fast once word got out and Wild Spring Dunes has the exact same DNA.

## Next Week
### Your Thursday Edition of The Turn

We have got a great trip itinerary we are sharing next week that you are going to want to book. Stay tuned.

See you next Thursday. 

Beau
excerpt: The family behind Bandon Dunes and Sand Valley just opened their newest course in the piney woods of East Texas and public tee times open for requests next week.
author: Beau
